input_prompt: create a lemlist campaign for VPs of Sales at Series B SaaS companies in Europe that are hiring Account Executives. Our product helps RevOps teams cut quota ramp time from 6 months to 3. 50 leads, paused.
campaign_title: [Yalc] VP Sales Series B SaaS EU – AE hiring trigger

active_signals: hiring Account Executive in last 30 days

pains_identified: AE quota ramp eats 6 months of runway
Inconsistent enablement playbooks across new hires
CRO scrutiny on cost per ramped AE

value_props: Cut AE quota ramp from 6 months to 3
Enablement playbooks that compound across hires
Lower cost per ramped AE — measured, not promised
offer: We compress new AE quota ramp from 6 months to 3 by automating the enablement playbook and surfacing the 5 deal-stage moves your top reps actually make.

competitors.name: Mindtickle
competitors.differentiation: We instrument the actual deal-stage moves, not just curriculum completion.

competitors.name: Gong
competitors.differentiation: We act on the call data, not just surface it.

triggers: Active AE job posting on LinkedIn in last 30 days

leads.angle: AE hiring spike + Series B runway pressure — quota ramp acceleration is now a board-level metric
Post-Series B sales build-out — playbook standardization across new EMEA hires
Hiring AEs + new enablement lead — perfect timing for systematized ramp
leads.trigger: Hiring 2 AEs + new sales enablement lead

sequence_steps.subject: AE ramp at {{companyName}}
sequence_steps.body: Hi {{firstName}}, noticed you're hiring AEs at {{companyName}}. Most VPs we talk to lose 6 months of runway to AE ramp. We compress that to 3 by surfacing the 5 deal-stage moves your top reps actually make — not curriculum completion. Worth comparing notes?

Quick follow up — for {{companyName}}'s Series B build-out, the playbook standardization piece tends to be what compounds across the next 3-4 hires. Happy to share the framework even if our timing is off.
Last one from me — if AE ramp isn't a current priority, all good. If it is, the 3-month benchmark is a 15-minute walkthrough.

top_improvement_notes: Step 2 has no subject (thread reply) — confirm sender's email client renders the thread correctly.
Step 1 CTA could be more action-oriented; consider 'open to comparing notes?' instead of 'Worth a chat?'
gtm_action_thinker_challenge: Weakest assumption: that VPs read cold email at all. Mitigation: shorter follow-ups and consider adding a LinkedIn touch as a separate companion campaign.

mcp_call_plan.notes: Enrichment flags OFF by default. Sequential, single-retry on 429/5xx, log to failed_leads[] on second failure. Stop if failed_count > 20% of succeeded_count.

instruction: Type 'approve' in chat to push this campaign to lemlist. Type anything else to abort. Campaign will be created in DRAFT state — orchestrator never calls set_campaign_state(start). You start the campaign manually in lemlist UI after review.
